full_text:
https://pbs.twimg.com/media/EpcfEYdW8AIDkBn.jpg

We aim to develop a novel technique, ProteoPicking, leveraging the advantages of t-CycIF and MS-based proteomics. This method has the potential to unveil structural, functional, and PTM proteomic landscapes within the tumor microenvironment with an unprecedented spatial precision.

https://pbs.twimg.com/media/EpcfEYdW8AIDkBn.jpg

ProteoPicking: bridging t-CycIF with precision tissue microdissection for TME proteomic exploration Lina Maltrovsky1, Ada Junquera1, Saundarya Shah1, Matilda Salko1, Anni Härkkonen1 2, Maija Väärikoski1 2,
Ulla-Maija Haltia1 2, Otto Kauko, Anniina Färkkilä 1 2 4 1 Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland
2 Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland
3 Turku Bioscience Centre, University of Turku & Åbo Akademi
4 iCAN digital precision cancer medicine, Helsinki, Finland; Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland, Helsinki Institute of Life Sciences, University of Helsinki, Finland

Figure 1: Schematic overview of the method.

t-CycIF does not affect the number of proteins in the tissue in comparison to simple IF staining

Proteomics of tumor and stroma cells differ from one another – the average amount of identified proteins in the tumor is higher than in the stroma

The number of picks correlates with the number of identified proteins

Optimizing the method by - reducing the amount of impurities in the samples - reducing number of necessary picks
Creating a pipeline for the effective analysis of the proteomics raw data - Differential Protein Expression Analysis - Pathway Enrichment Analysis
Comparing proteomics of tumor, tumor-stroma interface (TSI), and stroma 
Comparing proteomics of proliferating cells with senescent cells (p21 high Ki67– pRB–) 
Mapping picking sites on the t-CycIF images

Contact information: Lina Maltrovsky, lina.maltrovsky@helsinki.fi

Aim

Tissue Cyclic ImmunoFluorescence(t-CycIF)5

Highly multiplexed imaging technique
AB-panel based
Performed with FFPE tissue sections
Reveals structural & functional features
Tissue analysis at spatial resolution

Mass Spectrometry (MS)-based proteomics6

Analyses proteins on a large scale
Not panel-based
Information on structure, functions, roles, & post translational modifications (PTM)

Analyses proteins on a large scale
Not panel-based
Information on structure, functions, roles, & post translational modifications (PTM)
Enables insight into cellular processes & disease mechanisms
